content: The iterative search-driven knowledge scaffolding process used to build out fragmented understanding of both self-supervised world model memory agents and general agent design can be reframed as a microcosm of the 2025 self-supervised world model memory agent’s own memory formation mechanism: just as researchers assemble disjoint partial search findings into a coherent working model of agent functionality, the 2025 memory agent actively curates fragmented unlabeled world experience into predictive, retrievable memory schemas via self-supervised bootstrapping. This framing reorients how we evaluate and design agent memory, treating the agent’s active exploration for novel experience as equivalent to a researcher’s iterative search for missing domain knowledge.
